title: antirrhinum majus subsp. latifolium
intro: Antirrhinum majus subsp. latifolium, also known as the broad-leaved snapdragon, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Plantaginaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and aiding in digestion. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Antirrhinum majus subsp. latifolium are often made into teas, infusions, or used in tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
